structure_description: 16-section consumer segmentation report. Front matter (cover/methodology/TOC) → executive summary → dark divider → three analytical segmentation cuts (demographic, behavioral, psychographic) → three single-page persona_profile pages → channel preference → willingness-to-pay → brand affinity → methodology endnote. Persona pages use a new page_type (persona_profile) — see manifest.yaml for slot spec.

sections.purpose: Report cover — title, country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag
sections.notes: No content generation needed — pure template fill from parsed topic metadata. Title pattern often: '{{country}}'s {{industry}} consumer — segmentation {{year}}'.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview of research approach: panel surveys, statistics bureau anchors, in-depth interviews, NPS / brand-affinity panels, AI-augmented synthesis, source tagging conventions. Call out triangulation for WTP figures specifically.
Table of contents — generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Dark-mode chapter break — single thesis statement that frames the segmentation lens. Example: 'Three axes, three personas, one decision tree.'

Two-page executive summary. Page 1: 4 callouts (TAM consumers in millions, dominant segment share of value, biggest behavioral shift, NPS leader or affinity index) + 2-col narrative + 1 chart (segment value share). Page 2: 5 strategic implications grid — what each segment shift means for market participants.
sections.char_budget_hint: callout num_max=12 chars, narrative_left total_chars_max=1200, implications cards body_max=360 chars each

sections.purpose: Demographic dissection — stacked chart of consumer population by age band × income tier × urban/peri-urban/rural geography. Sidebar narrative on which demographic cells command outsize share of category value. Identifies the 'who' axis.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = 3-axis stacked bar or heatmap (age × income with geo as small-multiples). Narrative = 2 paragraphs flagging the top 2 cells by value contribution.

sections.purpose: Behavioral dissection — 2×2 or 3×3 matrix of purchase frequency × basket size, overlaid with loyalty index (NPS / repeat-rate / switching probability). Sidebar narrative on which behavioral cell drives category profit pool.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = bubble matrix (X=frequency, Y=basket, size=loyalty). 4 quadrant callouts naming each cell.

sections.purpose: Psychographic dissection — clustering on values (status / utility / sustainability / community / convenience) × lifestyle (digital-native, family-anchored, career-first, etc.). Charts cluster centroids. This page sets up the three persona pages that follow.
sections.skip_if: topic has no available consumer values data (e.g. ultra-niche B2C with no panel coverage) — collapse psychographic commentary into behavioral page instead, drop this section, and route one extra persona page in.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = 2D cluster scatter with 3 highlighted clusters labeled to match the personas in 09/10/11. Narrative bridges into persona pages.

sections.purpose: Single-page deep profile of the largest-value persona. Combines demo stats grid (4 stats), behavior grid (4 cards), one quote callout, channel preference horizontal bar, and a WTP anchor figure. See manifest.yaml for the full persona_profile slot spec.
sections.char_budget_hint: label cap 30, headline cap 70, demo stats 4× (num 12 / unit 8 / label 30), behavior cards 4× (metric 14 / label 30 / change-line 38), quote 180 + attribution 40, channel_pref_caption 110, WTP num 12 + unit 8 + change-line 38.

sections.purpose: Single-page deep profile of the highest-growth persona — the segment expanding fastest in share or in absolute spend.
Single-page deep profile of the highest-WTP-or-most-strategic persona — premium tier, niche-defender, or a contested segment with structural switching exposure.

Cross-segment channel mix — stacked bar showing % spend across modern trade / traditional / e-commerce / social commerce / direct, broken out per persona (3 bars). Sidebar narrative on where channel-shift catalysts are concentrated.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = 3 stacked horizontal bars (one per persona). 2-paragraph narrative on the directional shift (typically social commerce vs. modern trade).

sections.purpose: Price-sensitivity curves per persona — Van Westendorp or simple demand-curve sketch. Anchored WTP figures (USD or local currency) per persona with elasticity commentary. Includes a callout grid flagging which persona is the price-anchor versus the premium-payer.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = 3 demand-curve traces or a clustered bar of WTP bands. 4 callouts (one per persona + one category-wide anchor).

sections.purpose: NPS / brand-affinity index per persona, switching probability matrix (which brand loses share to which), and a short list of the 3-5 brands with disproportionate persona pull. No deep player profiles — this is segment-side analysis, not competitive landscape.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart = NPS bars per persona + a 3×3 switching-flow Sankey OR a top-5 brands affinity table. Narrative anchors which persona is most/least loyal.

sections.title_pattern: Five implications across the three personas
sections.purpose: Closing 5-card implications grid — one per major persona-anchored move (e.g. 'Price-anchor Persona 01 via {{format}}', 'Defend Persona 03 against social-commerce leakage', etc.). Reuses the exec_summary_p2_implications page type so we don't invent a third closing format.
sections.char_budget_hint: Cards body_max=360 chars each. Lead each card with an imperative verb addressed to market participants.

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary]/[secondary]/[estimate] percentages), panel composition disclosure (n=, geography, recruitment method), KIRA estimate methodologies disclosed (especially for WTP triangulation), persona-clustering note, contact & next research footer.
sections.notes: Required — buyers value transparent methodology, doubly so on persona-led work where the panel disclosure is the credibility anchor. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report.

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '012 / NN' in page-header counter slot — total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section (e.g. all three persona pages tagged 'Section 08 · Personas')
assembly_rules.divider_placement: One dark divider only — opens the segmentation chapter. Persona pages do NOT each get a divider (would push page count over budget).
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if triggers (only on 08_psychographic in default config), omit the section AND recompute page counter. If psychographic drops, consider promoting a 4th persona page using the freed slot — orchestrator decision.

page_type_notes.persona_profile: NEW page type introduced by this blueprint. Slot spec lives in manifest.yaml under 'persona_profile page type — slot spec'. When the persona_profile schema is formalized into schemas/page_schemas.json, port the caps from manifest into that file and remove this inline doc. Until then, the manifest comment is authoritative.
